checks.limitation: Cannot claim zero from connection-source inventory alone.

checks.evidence: Connection-level sources map to existing terms, but v2.12 uses the more specific topographic-sector classification.
checks.limitation: Representation is recorded in the topographic-sector row.

checks.evidence: v2.11/v2.12 maps the mixed contribution to existing boundary/profile/topographic/screening/lift sectors.
checks.limitation: This closes R_bundle for the mixed contribution but does not prove the full H_T theorem.

checks.evidence: No symmetry and PSD proof for R_bundle is present.
checks.limitation: The PSD/profile package cannot absorb an unidentified sign-indefinite curvature contraction.

checks.evidence: No proof excludes R_bundle from H_perp or lifts it above threshold.
checks.limitation: Screening/lifting remains unavailable without its sector/chirality action.

checks.evidence: No constants a_R,b_R have been derived for ||R_bundle psi|| <= a_R ||A0 psi|| + b_R ||psi||.
checks.limitation: Relative-bound closure requires an explicit operator formula or norm estimate.

checks.evidence: The term is not proven nonzero or uncontrolled; only unresolved.
checks.limitation: Failure would require showing the term breaks lower-bound transfer.

checks.evidence: lichnerowicz_bundle_curvature_remainder has no complete formula/action; 4 connection sources retain curvature-remainder risk.
checks.limitation: This is an honest theorem gap, not an H_T theorem failure.

limitations: The audit classifies the remainder exactly once.
The v2.12 classification is topographic representation when the mixed contribution contributes no independent R_bundle term.
title: BHSM v2.7 Curvature Remainder Audit
